title: Cat Vaccine Schedule 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
h1: Cat Vaccination Schedule: FVRCP, Rabies & Leukemia
description: Plan your cat's vaccines: FVRCP (triple), rabies, and feline leukemia schedule. Free veterinary vaccination guide for 2026.
intro: Responsible cat care requires understanding vaccination schedules by age and health status. FVRCP (the triple vaccine), rabies, and feline leukemia vaccines protect against serious diseases. This free calculator provides a vaccination timeline based on 2026 veterinary guidelines, updated regularly to keep you informed.
keyTakeaway: Cat vaccination schedule calculator covering FVRCP (triple vaccine), rabies, and feline leukemia. Free tool to plan your kitten or adult cat's complete immunization timeline.
useCases: Pet owners preparing for veterinary appointments
Tracking booster doses and revaccination timing
explanation:
Responsible pet care follows international veterinary guidelines (WSAVA, AAHA). Nutrition, vaccination, and parasite control have specific protocols based on species, breed, weight, and age.

## How Cat Vaccination Works

Feline vaccination schedules include three key vaccines: FVRCP (the triple vaccine protecting against feline viral rhinotracheitis, calicivirus, and panleukopenia), rabies, and feline leukemia. Kittens typically start at 6-8 weeks and receive boosters every 3-4 weeks until 16 weeks of age. Adult cats require regular boosters as recommended by your veterinarian.

## How This Calculator Works

This free calculator helps you understand your cat's vaccination timeline based on standard veterinary protocols. Enter your cat's age and vaccination history to see recommended next steps. The tool flags any unusual situations requiring veterinary consultation.

## Important Notes

This calculator provides guidance only and is not a substitute for professional veterinary advice. For any health decisions, consult with your veterinarian. Information is current as of 2026 and updated regularly.

faq.q: When should I start vaccinating my kitten?
faq.a: Kittens should receive their first vaccines at 6-8 weeks of age, followed by boosters every 3-4 weeks until 16 weeks old.

faq.q: What vaccines does my cat need?
faq.a: Core vaccines include FVRCP (triple vaccine) and rabies. Non-core vaccines like feline leukemia (FeLV) depend on lifestyle and risk factors.

faq.q: How often does my cat need booster shots?
faq.a: After the initial series, cats typically need booster shots annually or every 3 years depending on the vaccine and your veterinarian's recommendation.
FVRCP protects against feline viral rhinotracheitis, calicivirus, and panleukopenia. It's commonly called the 'triple vaccine.'

faq.q: Does my indoor cat need rabies vaccination?
faq.a: Yes, rabies vaccination is recommended for all cats, even indoor ones. It's required by law in most areas and provides protection if exposure occurs.
Cats are considered fully vaccinated after completing their initial kitten series and receiving recommended booster shots according to your veterinarian's schedule.
This calculator provides general guidance based on standard veterinary protocols. Always consult your veterinarian for your specific cat's vaccination needs.
